mnemonics.title: UCCK - Union Concatenation Closed
mnemonics.content: UCCK: Union, Concatenation, Closure (Kleene star) are Closed for CFLs. Remember: UCCK = You See C (context-free) K (keep closed)

mnemonics.title: INP - Intersection Not Property
mnemonics.content: INP: Intersection, Not (complement), Product (cartesian product) - Not closed. Remember: 'INP = Not in proper closure'

acronyms.usage: Languages generated by CFGs and recognized by PDA
Grammar where left side is single non-terminal
Automaton with stack memory used to recognize CFLs
Languages recognized by deterministic PDA, closed under complement

visualTips.concept: CFL closure under intersection with regular
visualTips.visualization: Draw a PDA for CFL with a box, draw a DFA for regular with a box, connect output of PDA to input of DFA - show the intersection works by running PDA and checking acceptance with DFA
Use Venn diagram showing two overlapping CFL circles with the intersection region marked as 'May not be CFL'
Draw concentric circles: innermost = Regular, middle = CFL, outermost = CSL, with operations listed at boundaries showing which preserve each class

analogies.analogy: Like different building materials - union is like combining two building sets (still a complete set), intersection is like finding common pieces between two different LEGO sets (might result in something that doesn't fit together nicely)
Think of CFL as a complex recipe and regular language as a simple filter - running a complex recipe through any filter still gives you something from the complex recipe family
